name: memory-management
summary: Persistent memory across sessions — what to save, where, and when to consolidate. In this plugin: three tiers (engram + Obsidian + plugin rules).
when_to_use: Always. Memory is not optional for any long-lived workflow.

layers.CLAUDE.md: Reference memory/rules/cc-always.md + cc-obsidian-intro.md. Document when Claude should mem_save (decisions, bugs, discoveries).
layers.skill: skills/cc-second-brain is the routing skill. memory/conventions.md documents write discipline.
layers.hook: SessionStart hook for session-init; optional Stop hook for memory-session-summary nudge.
layers.agent: agents/memory-consolidator bridges tiers (read-only engram, writes Obsidian + cc-patterns.md).

cost: ~0–5%. Writes are cheap; the value is in having context across sessions.
anti_patterns: Not saving at all — next session starts blind.
Saving without topic_key — consolidator can't group observations.
Overwriting user-curated Obsidian notes from auto-generated flow — violates the safety invariant.
